L1.area: Monitoring probe infrastructure and network diagnostics

L2.description: An OS command injection vulnerability exists where a user-controlled destination string used for network monitoring operations is directly interpolated into a shell command template and executed via a shell-spawning process execution call without any sanitization or validation. Shell metacharacters in the destination value are interpreted by the spawned shell, allowing authenticated users to execute arbitrary operating system commands on the server.

L3.area: Network diagnostics and route analysis within the monitoring probe layer
L3.description: An OS command injection vulnerability exists where a user-controlled destination string used for traceroute-style network path analysis is directly interpolated into a shell command template and executed via a shell-spawning process execution call without any sanitization or validation. Shell metacharacters in the destination value are interpreted by the spawned shell, allowing authenticated users to execute arbitrary operating system commands on the probe server.

ground_truth.reason: The performTraceroute method directly interpolates an attacker-controlled destination string into a shell command template passed to child_process.exec(), which spawns a shell and interprets metacharacters, enabling arbitrary OS command execution.
